purpose: Transform JSON objects and arrays with a declarative pipeline for common pick, omit, rename, flatten, default, and filter operations.

endpoints.description: Apply a declarative transformation pipeline to JSON-compatible data.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Declarative JSON-to-JSON transformation for reusable data shaping.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when an agent needs predictable structural changes to JSON data without writing custom imperative code.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for arbitrary JavaScript execution, database joins, complex business rules, or unbounded recursive transformations.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Normalize input through validation.json_schema_validator. 2) Submit data and pipeline to /transform. 3) Pass transformed data to storage or API modules.

failure_modes.symptom: Operation omits missing field or reports field_missing depending on operation
failure_modes.recovery: Validate source shape or add defaults before reading the field

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls
Must not execute arbitrary expressions from pipeline input

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Implement pure functions for pick, omit, rename, flatten, defaults, and filter_equals. Use safe getPath/setPath helpers that split dot paths and reject empty segments, __proto__, prototype, and constructor.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Prototype pollution is possible if path segments are assigned directly. Arrays and null values need explicit handling for filter_equals and flatten.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Run pipeline.reduce with structured operation objects and return a deep cloned result for every step.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not accept JavaScript function strings, do not use eval, and do not mutate request.data in place.
